Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x49FE "RISK-7FA0-49FE" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 jsr $7fa0 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Risk.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
Add an init routine at $7FA0 which: 1. Sets up the correct VICII display settings 2. Copies data from memory starting at $8131 to colour RAM
Note: As there was no free space left in memory to place a compressed copy of colour RAM for the title screen, the lower nibbles from colour RAM were stored in the bytes starting at $8131. This part of memory stores map data from the game and was chosen because every other byte stores a value less than $0F. This meant that the existing lower nibbles (4 bits) of these values could be left shifted into the higher nibble, thus allowing the 4-bit colour RAM data to be stored in the lower nibble of each byte. This process is reversed when the game is loaded, returning the map data to it's original state.
This small bit of code can be used to store the colour RAM nibbles in every other byte starting at $8131 before taking the dump:
SOURCEPTR = $FB DESTPTR = $FD TEMP = $2A
*=$7FBC
; Set source address in $FA to Colour RAM (D800) lda #$d8 sta SOURCEPTR+1 lda #$00 sta SOURCEPTR
; Set destination address in $FC lda #$81 sta DESTPTR+1 lda #$31 sta DESTPTR
ldx #$04 ldy #$00 src lda (SOURCEPTR),Y and #$0f sta TEMP lda (DESTPTR),Y asl asl asl asl ora TEMP sta (DESTPTR),Y
inc SOURCEPTR bne noinc inc SOURCEPTR+1
dex beq done
noinc lda DESTPTR clc adc #$02 sta DESTPTR bcc src
inc DESTPTR+1 bcs src
done jmp *
